claims.evidence_text: at harvest stage, it varied from 65.54 cm to 84.68 cm based on two years mean values
claims.claim_summary: Wheat plant height at harvest ranged 65.54-84.68 cm across irrigation treatments (two-year mean).

claims.evidence_text: The maximum plant height at 30, 60, 90 DAS and harvest stages was 17.77, 66.41, 76.63 and 84.68 cm, respectively.
claims.claim_summary: Maximum wheat plant height at harvest (4 irrigations: CRI, tillering, jointing, milking) was 84.68 cm.

claims.evidence_text: minimum plant height was notated when only one irrigation was provided at the CRI Stage, the height being 15.64, 54.11, 58.46, and 65.54 cm
claims.claim_summary: Minimum wheat plant height at harvest (single irrigation at CRI) was 65.54 cm.

claims.evidence_text: wheat was irrigated at cumulative pan evaporation (CPE) of 50, 75, 100 or 125 mm. Grain yield increased from 2.07 t ha-1 with irrigation at 125 mm CPE to 3.23 t ha-1 with irrigation at 50 mm CPE.
claims.claim_summary: Wheat irrigated by cumulative pan evaporation of 50-125 mm; highest grain yield at 50 mm CPE scheduling.

claims.evidence_text: four times irrigations given to wheat crop at CRI, tillering, jointing and milking stages resulted in almost significantly higher plant height at every stage of observations
claims.claim_summary: Critical wheat irrigation stages are CRI (crown root initiation), tillering, jointing, and milking; irrigating these maximizes growth.
